questions.q: Who launched the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920?
questions.explanation: Gandhi launched the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920.

questions.q: In which year did the Non-Cooperation Movement begin?
questions.explanation: The movement was launched in 1920.

questions.q: The Non-Cooperation Movement asked Indians to boycott British what?
questions.explanation: It called for the boycott of British schools, courts, titles, and goods.

questions.q: The Non-Cooperation Movement transformed the freedom struggle into what kind of movement?
questions.explanation: It turned the struggle into a mass movement involving millions of Indians.

questions.q: Which of these were Indians urged to surrender during the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.explanation: Indians were urged to renounce titles and honours conferred by the British.

questions.q: The Non-Cooperation Movement was based on which Gandhian principle?
questions.explanation: The movement rested on Gandhi's principle of non-violent resistance.

questions.q: Which handspun cloth was promoted as part of the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.explanation: The movement promoted khadi, the handspun cloth, as a symbol of self-reliance.

questions.q: Students were encouraged to leave what during the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.explanation: Students were urged to boycott government-run schools and colleges.

questions.q: What was meant to be burned in public bonfires during the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.explanation: Foreign cloth was burned in public bonfires as part of the boycott.

questions.q: The Non-Cooperation Movement was an effort to withdraw Indian what from British rule?
questions.explanation: Its core idea was to withdraw Indian cooperation and consent from the colonial government.

questions.q: At which 1920 Congress session was the Non-Cooperation programme adopted?
questions.explanation: The Nagpur Congress session of December 1920 formally adopted Non-Cooperation.

questions.q: The Non-Cooperation Movement combined with which Muslim-led agitation?
questions.explanation: It was launched in alliance with the Khilafat movement.

questions.q: Which two 'wrongs' did Gandhi cite as reasons for launching Non-Cooperation?
options: The salt and land tax wrongs
The Khilafat and Punjab (Jallianwala Bagh) wrongs
questions.explanation: Gandhi cited the Khilafat issue and the Punjab wrong of Jallianwala Bagh.

questions.q: The visit of which royal commission or person was boycotted during the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1921?
questions.explanation: The Prince of Wales's 1921 visit was met with boycotts and a hartal.

questions.q: Which honorific title did Gandhi himself renounce as part of the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.explanation: Gandhi returned the Kaiser-i-Hind medal he had received for his South Africa service.

questions.q: Which 1922 incident led Gandhi to call off the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.explanation: Gandhi suspended the movement after the violence at Chauri Chaura in February 1922.

questions.q: At Chauri Chaura, which building did the mob set ablaze, prompting the movement's withdrawal?
questions.explanation: An angry crowd set fire to a police station at Chauri Chaura, killing policemen inside.

questions.q: Many lawyers, including Motilal Nehru and C. R. Das, did what during the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.explanation: Prominent lawyers gave up lucrative practices to support the boycott of courts.

questions.q: Which national educational institution was founded in 1920 amid the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.explanation: Jamia Millia Islamia was founded in 1920 as a national institution during the movement.

questions.q: Gandhi promised that Swaraj could be attained within what timeframe if the movement succeeded?
questions.explanation: Gandhi famously promised Swaraj within one year if Non-Cooperation was fully carried out.

questions.q: Which Congress leader, later a Forward Bloc founder, resigned a top civil-service path to join such struggles?
questions.explanation: Subhas Chandra Bose resigned from the Indian Civil Service to join the national movement.

questions.q: The Non-Cooperation Movement saw a major boycott of which 1920 colonial reform institution?
questions.explanation: Indians were urged to boycott the councils set up under the 1919 reforms.

questions.q: In which present-day district is Chauri Chaura, where the movement was halted, located?
questions.explanation: Chauri Chaura lies in the Gorakhpur district of Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: On what date in 1922 did the Chauri Chaura incident occur, leading to the movement's suspension?
questions.explanation: The Chauri Chaura incident took place on 4 February 1922.

questions.q: After calling off Non-Cooperation, Gandhi was arrested and tried in 1922 in which case?
options: The Great Trial at Ahmedabad for sedition
questions.explanation: Gandhi was tried for sedition in the 1922 'Great Trial' at Ahmedabad and sentenced to six years.

questions.q: Which Congress committee earlier drafted the Non-Cooperation resolution at the 1920 Calcutta special session?
options: The special session presided over by Lala Lajpat Rai
questions.explanation: The Calcutta special session of September 1920, presided over by Lala Lajpat Rai, first approved Non-Cooperation.

questions.q: The Non-Cooperation Movement encouraged the spread of which arbitration bodies to replace British courts?
questions.explanation: People were urged to settle disputes through panchayats and national tribunals instead of British courts.

questions.q: Which Assam tea-garden episode of 1921 became a notable event linked to the Non-Cooperation period?
options: The Chandpur exodus of plantation labourers
questions.explanation: The 1921 exodus of Assam tea-garden workers and the Chandpur firing became flashpoints during the movement.

questions.q: Which Akali agitation in Punjab ran parallel to the Non-Cooperation Movement in the early 1920s?
questions.explanation: The Akali movement for gurdwara reform in Punjab overlapped with Non-Cooperation in the early 1920s.

questions.q: Disillusioned Congressmen who wanted to enter councils after the movement's end formed which party?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru and C. R. Das founded the Swaraj Party in 1923 to contest council elections after Non-Cooperation ended.
